title: Failure-Code Registry Entry
description: One entry in the unified failure-code registry (rules/codes.md, schema'd frontmatter; spec §7.3, §10.2). Every code emitted on any artifact MUST exist here; CI validates. Field names and family/tier/disposition tokens are contract, not instance data.

code.description: The dotted code token (e.g. LINT.EM_DASH, FM.HOOK_WEAK, PKG.RECOMMENDED_MISSING, PLAT.TWITTER_HASHTAG_PRESENT, VIS.OFF_BRAND, SYS.READBACK_FAIL).

family.description: Namespaced family mapping the production source layer onto a single contract (spec §10.2).

tier.description: Severity tier; hard always implies disposition block.

source.description: The gate layer that emits this code.

disposition.description: Consequence when the code fires (DD-20; hard ⇒ block; v1 default ruleset ships all soft codes as warn, RD-21).

route.description: Which seat receives a routed-back failure for this code (spec §10.2; §9.2 hard-fail routing).

description.description: Human-readable definition of what the code means.

rule_ref.description: Id of the rule file that documents and (for deterministic checks) enforces this code (spec §10.1 rule/code pairing).
